feed.title: Field Notes by Larry Rawlings
feed.subtitle: Light, location, and the working life of a Charlotte photographer

entry.title: Why I Slowed Down and Went Back to Film
entry.summary: Every frame costing you changes how you see. Notes on returning to a manual body for personal work.

entry.title: The Half Hour Before the Porch Light Comes On
entry.summary: A year into the series, the project has taught me more about patience than about light.

entry.title: What a Corporate Gala Actually Asks of You
entry.summary: Reading a room, working the media wall, and staying invisible while everyone else celebrates.

entry.title: Packing One Body and Two Lenses for a City You Have to Sit In
entry.summary: How I plan a trip around light and walking, not around a checklist of sights.
